title: The Deflation Spiral
lineage: MINIMALIST SPACE LOGISTICS · M1 — the money macro

scenes.narration: Minimalist Space Logistics. The deflation spiral: a war economy can starve with full warehouses, because the money to pay for the goods has pooled and stopped moving.

scenes.headline: Money is never minted at the point of sale.
scenes.body: Every credit you earn was sitting in someone else's account a tick ago. So the only question that matters is: is it still circulating — or has it pooled, and died?
scenes.narration: Money is never minted when you sell. Every credit you earn was in someone else's account a tick ago. So: is it still circulating, or has it pooled and died?

scenes.kicker: five money moves · every tick · double-entry
scenes.headline: One source. Money flows down, and leaks back up.
scenes.narration: Each tick, money makes five moves. A war bond mints credits into the treasury, the only source. It pays you bounties; you trade against the tills; the front buys output, pushing money down; and a carrying cost bleeds every till back up.

scenes.headline: The upward leak is what makes the spiral real.
scenes.body: If money only ever flowed down, it could never pool. The leak turns direction into a race — drain up versus pump down. Throttle the pump and the leak wins: money climbs into the treasury and goes idle, where it can't pay anyone.
scenes.narration: That upward leak is the whole trick. If money only flowed down, it could never pool. The leak makes direction a race. Throttle the pump, and the leak wins: money climbs into the treasury and goes idle.

scenes.headline: Three counter-forces keep the blood moving.
bullets: The war-bond tick — a guaranteed external source. The heartbeat.
The front pays the tills — the pump that pushes hoarded money back down.
The treasury floor — a backstop that forces a minimum board so bounties never fully stop.
scenes.narration: Three guards keep the blood pumping: the war bond, a guaranteed source; the front paying the tills, the pump that pushes money back down; and a treasury floor, the backstop that keeps bounties alive.

scenes.headline: Cross the line and the regime flips.
scenes.narration: Plot the source against circulation, and the economy splits in two: healthy up-right, spiral down-left. Cross the line and velocity collapses, the tills drain to zero, and the board goes quiet.

scenes.headline: The books always balanced. The money never left.
scenes.body: Through the whole collapse, the conservation light stays green. The total never changed — the money was always there. It had just stopped moving. Velocity is the vital sign.
scenes.narration: Yet through the whole collapse, the books stay balanced. The money never left. It just stopped moving. Velocity is the vital sign.

scenes.headline: Drive it yourself.
bullets: Play / Step / Reset — run the deterministic tick or single-step it (seed 4071).
The dials — war-bond size, front-pays-tills rate, treasury spend, the carrying-cost leak.
The three guard toggles — switch the war-bond, circulation, or treasury floor off.
Trigger spiral · Deploy a guard · Naïve model — one-click demos of collapse and recovery.
scenes.narration: Drive it yourself. Press Play to run the deterministic tick, or Step through it. Work the dials: war-bond size, the front-pays-tills rate, and the carrying-cost leak. Flip the three guards off one at a time, or hit the one-click demos to trigger a spiral and then deploy a guard to break it.

scenes.headline: The Deflation Spiral
scenes.body: Deterministic · seeded · double-entry integer credits · a live explorable you can poke yourself.
scenes.narration: Deterministic, seeded, and built on double-entry integer credits. A live explorable you can poke yourself.
